summary: Wormian bones indicate normal skull variation instead of indicative evidence of physical trauma or abuse; thus, they should not be considered when assessing potential cases of domestic violence in pediatric patients based primarily on skeletal findings alone. Other critical markers like unexplained bone fractures provide stronger clues towards suspicion of non-accidental injury requiring further investigation into possible domestic violence scenarios involving minors. Agent2 concludes that 'wormian' is the exception among listed options due its lack of association with suspected abuse-related conditions compared against more definitive red flags present elsewhere within medical records concerning abused infants/children. This distinction helps narrow down diagnostic considerations focusing attention appropriately onto high-risk symptoms warranting immediate intervention measures aimed at protecting vulnerable young formatted_summary: agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Wormian bones indicate normal skull variation instead of indicative evidence of physical trauma or abuse; thus, they should not be considered when assessing potential cases of domestic violence in pediatric patients based primarily on skeletal findings alone. Other critical markers like unexplained bone fractures provide stronger clues towards suspicion of non-accidental injury requiring further investigation into possible domestic violence scenarios involving minors. Agent2 concludes that 'wormian' is the exception among listed options due its lack of association with suspected abuse-related conditions compared against more definitive red flags present elsewhere within medical records concerning abused infants/children. This distinction helps narrow down diagnostic considerations focusing attention appropriately onto high-risk symptoms warranting immediate intervention measures aimed at protecting vulnerable young
